name: Cheddar Braids
recipeInstructions:
To make the dough:
1. Pour the warm water into a bowl and add the yeast. Stir to dissolve, then let stand until light and puffed, about 5 minutes.
2. Add 1 1/2 cups of flour, sugar, and salt to the yeast mixture. Beat with an electric mixer on the lowest speed for 1 minute.
3. Continue beating on medium speed for 2 minutes longer.
4. Add the butter to the yeast mixture and beat for another 1 minute.
5. Beat in 1 egg and 1/2 cup of flour at a time until well blended, using all 4 eggs and adding enough flour to make a soft, sticky dough.
6. Beat with the mixer or by hand until glossy and elastic, pulling away from the bowl's sides.
7. Stir in the cheddar cheese by hand. Cover and let rise in a warm place free from drafts until doubled in bulk, about 2 1/2 to 3 hours.
To prepare for baking:
8. When dough has doubled in size, punch down and refrigerate at least 5 hours or better overnight.
9. Remove the dough from the refrigerator. Divide into half and cover, then refrigerate the second ball of dough.
10. Knead the remaining dough on a lightly floured surface until soft and pliable.
11. Divide the dough into 3 equal parts and roll each piece into a rope, 12 to 16 inches long.
12. Braid the ropes, starting in the middle and working toward each end. Pinch the ends together to seal them.
To finish:
13. Grease a large baking sheet and place the finished braid on one side of the sheet. Repeat with the refrigerated dough.
14. In a small bowl, beat the egg and milk together.
15. Brush the braids with the egg mixture and let rise in a warm place free from drafts until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
16. Do not cover midway through the rising time; brush again with the egg mixture.
17. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
18. When fully risen, brush with the egg mixture and sprinkle evenly with celery seeds.
19. Bake for 40 minutes in the preheated oven until a wooden skewer or pick inserted comes out dry. Remove from the oven and baking sheet, then cool to room temperature on wire racks before slicing.
